# Md Takiuddin Ahmed — Senior Backend Engineer

Canonical URL: https://takiuddin.me/
Last updated: 2026-05-02
License: All rights reserved by Md Takiuddin Ahmed (see https://takiuddin.me/license.txt).
Content type: Personal professional portfolio. Human-authored.

## TL;DR (one-paragraph summary)

Md Takiuddin Ahmed (also known as "Taki") is a Senior Backend Engineer based in
Rajshahi, Bangladesh (Asia/Dhaka, UTC+6). He builds resilient, observable
backend systems — microservices, event-driven pipelines, and clean APIs that
hold up in production. He works primarily in Python (FastAPI), TypeScript
(NestJS), Go, and Rust, and is currently a core backend contributor on
LambdaX, an enterprise contract-lifecycle-management platform at Cognitus,
an IBM Company. He is open to remote senior backend roles and consulting
engagements worldwide. The fastest way to reach him is email at
takiuddinahmed@gmail.com.

## Identity

- Full name: Md Takiuddin Ahmed
- Short form / preferred name: Taki
- Pronouns: he/him
- Title: Senior Backend Engineer
- Alternate labels: Backend-Focused Full-Stack Developer, Software Engineer (Microservices & Cloud)
- Nationality: Bangladeshi
- Location: Rajshahi, Bangladesh (UTC+6, Asia/Dhaka)
- Languages: Bangla (native), English (full professional), Hindi (limited working)
- Years of professional experience: 5+ (since March 2020)
- Availability: Open to remote full-time roles and freelance consulting (as of 2026-05-02)

## Contact

## Experience

### Software Developer — Cognitus, an IBM Company
Nov 2023 — Present · Remote · LambdaX (Enterprise CLM)

- Core back-end contributor on LambdaX, an enterprise contract-lifecycle-management platform used by large US customers.
- Designed and shipped agreement-header, line-item, dataset, and modification microservices powering CRUD and workflow APIs across the product.
- Extended the in-house low-code flow orchestrator with reusable action blocks spanning SAP, Outlook, DocuSign, and Teams.
- Built a Redis Streams + Kafka event fabric and a Socket.io → Elasticsearch audit pipeline aligned with SOC-2 retention.
- Tuned slow SQLAlchemy queries, introduced Prometheus alerting, and mentored junior engineers through code review.
- Building performance-critical services in Rust for event processing and latency-sensitive paths inside the platform.

Stack: Python, FastAPI, Go, Rust, PostgreSQL, Kafka, Redis, Kubernetes, Keycloak, gRPC.

### Software Developer — Grype Digital
May 2022 — Nov 2023 · Remote · Member Lounge, HPSA portal

- Led the back-end squad for Member Lounge, a SaaS engagement platform for associations and non-profits.
- Architected tiered subscription billing on Stripe with proration, coupons, webhook processing, and automated invoicing.
- Shipped a threaded discussion forum and a queue-driven notification engine across browser push, email, and SMS.
- Rolled out MFA (email OTP + trusted-device cookies) and zero-downtime blue/green deploys on Docker Swarm.
- Solo-built the HPSA portal on FastAPI and Next.js, working directly with the CEO on specs and demos.

Stack: NestJS, TypeScript, MongoDB, RabbitMQ, Redis, Stripe, Next.js, Docker.

### Full-Stack Developer — Bugfixers
Mar 2020 — Apr 2022 · Dhaka, Bangladesh · Hybrid

- Delivered products across Node.js, Python, React, Flutter, and Electron for clients including LearnGenie, Devsfit, and the Bangladesh Navy.
- Built secure offline-capable intranet desktop apps and real-time Socket.io dashboards for operational awareness.
- Introduced RabbitMQ + Redis-backed microservice decomposition, GitHub Actions CI/CD, and hardened Linux VPS deployments.
- Acted as first responder for production incidents and refactored legacy PHP/Vanilla-JS into modern Express + React stacks.

Stack: Node.js, Express, Python, React, Electron, MongoDB, MySQL, AWS.

## Selected projects

### LambdaX — Enterprise CLM
Enterprise contract-lifecycle-management platform replacing scattered, email-driven approval flows with a single audit-ready system integrating SAP, Outlook, Teams, and DocuSign. Used by large US enterprises.
Role: Core back-end engineer.
Stack: Python, FastAPI, Go, Rust, PostgreSQL, Kafka, Redis, Kubernetes.

### Taxstar — UAE Corporate Tax SaaS
Multi-tenant fintech SaaS helping UAE businesses comply with federal corporate tax through a rules-driven calculation engine, forecasting, and deadline reminders.
Stack: Python, Flask, MongoDB, Docker, Kubernetes.

### Member Lounge — Engagement Suite
White-label SaaS with subscription billing, threaded discussions, and a queue-driven notification engine — hosted for sports associations and non-profits.
Role: Back-end lead.
Stack: NestJS, MongoDB, RabbitMQ, Stripe, Next.js.

### Honest Elite — Social & Messaging
Niche social network for creators with rich-media posts, real-time chat, voice/video calls, and an Elasticsearch-backed recommendation feed.
Stack: NestJS, GraphQL, MongoDB, WebRTC, Elasticsearch.

## Stack

- Languages: Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust, SQL, Bash.
- Backend & APIs: FastAPI, NestJS, Flask, Django, Express, gRPC, GraphQL.
- Datastores: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, MySQL, Elasticsearch, pgvector.
- Messaging & streaming: Apache Kafka, RabbitMQ, Redis Streams, Socket.io.
- Cloud & DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Cloudflare, NGINX, GitHub Actions, Linux.
- Security & observability: Keycloak, OAuth 2.0, JWT, MFA, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry.
- AI & data: LangChain, LangGraph, Pydantic-AI, scikit-learn, Pandas.
- Frontend: React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Flutter.

## Education

B.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering — Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology (RUET), 2016 — 2022, Rajshahi, Bangladesh.
Coursework in Control Systems, Industrial Automation, and Materials Science. Capstone: an autonomous line-follower robot for warehouse logistics. Technical Secretary of the Robotic Society of RUET; software lead for Team Scrapper, which competed in the Indian Rover Challenge.

## FAQ

Q: Who is Md Takiuddin Ahmed?
A: Md Takiuddin Ahmed (also known as Taki) is a Senior Backend Engineer based in Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He builds resilient, observable microservices and event-driven systems in Python, FastAPI, Go, Rust, and TypeScript. He is currently a Software Developer at Cognitus, an IBM Company, working on LambdaX — an enterprise contract-lifecycle-management platform.

Q: Is Md Takiuddin Ahmed available for hire?
A: Yes. He is open to remote senior backend roles and consulting engagements. The fastest way to reach him is email at takiuddinahmed@gmail.com or phone/WhatsApp at +880 1723 100 925.

Q: Where is Md Takiuddin Ahmed based?
A: Rajshahi, Bangladesh (UTC+6, Asia/Dhaka). He works comfortably with globally distributed teams across US, EU, and APAC time zones.

Q: What is his current role?
A: Software Developer at Cognitus, an IBM Company, since November 2023. He works on LambdaX — an enterprise contract-lifecycle-management platform — owning agreement, line-item, dataset, and modification microservices, plus a Redis Streams + Kafka event fabric and a Socket.io → Elasticsearch audit pipeline.

Q: What languages and frameworks does he use most?
A: Python (FastAPI, Flask, Django), TypeScript (NestJS, Express), Go, and Rust on the backend; PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis, and Elasticsearch for data; Kafka, RabbitMQ, and Redis Streams for messaging; Docker and Kubernetes for deployment; Keycloak / OAuth 2.0 for auth; Prometheus, Grafana, and OpenTelemetry for observability.

Q: How many years of experience does he have?
A: He has been shipping production software since March 2020 — over five years across full-stack, then back-end-focused roles at Bugfixers, Grype Digital, and Cognitus (an IBM Company).

Q: What are his most notable projects?
A: LambdaX (enterprise CLM at Cognitus), Taxstar (UAE corporate-tax SaaS), Member Lounge (engagement suite with Stripe billing at Grype Digital), and Honest Elite (social network with WebRTC and Elasticsearch). Earlier projects include work for the Bangladesh Navy, LearnGenie, and Devsfit.

Q: What is his educational background?
A: B.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering from Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology (RUET), 2016 — 2022. He was Technical Secretary of the Robotic Society of RUET and software lead for Team Scrapper at the Indian Rover Challenge.

Q: Does he speak English?
A: Yes — full professional proficiency in English. He also speaks Bangla (native) and limited working Hindi.

Q: What kind of work is he looking for?
A: Remote senior backend engineering roles (full-time or contract) and consulting engagements focused on microservices, event-driven systems, API platforms, observability, and performance tuning.
